title: DaemonSet OnDelete Update Strategy
description:
Deploy a DaemonSet with `OnDelete` update strategy, update the container image, and manually trigger updates by deleting Pods one at a time.

**Focus**: Contrasting `OnDelete` with `RollingUpdate` — useful when you need to drain a node safely before replacing its agent.

## Common Pitfalls
- After `kubectl set image`, no Pods restart with OnDelete — this is correct, not a bug.
- Each Pod must be manually deleted to pick up the new image on its node.
- Verify the replacement Pod uses the new image before deleting the next one.
steps: Create a DaemonSet named 'log-agent' with OnDelete updateStrategy using busybox:1.35
Submit — the validator verifies OnDelete strategy is set, updates the image, confirms pods do NOT auto-update, then manually deletes one pod to verify the new pod starts with the updated image
hints: updateStrategy.type: OnDelete must be set inside spec, not spec.template
After kubectl set image, the StatefulSet DESIRED changes but pods do not restart — this is correct
kubectl get pods -l app=log-agent -o wide shows pod names and nodes
After deleting a pod, the DaemonSet creates a new one on the same node with the updated spec
kubectl describe ds log-agent shows the updateStrategy and current image in the spec
With OnDelete strategy, pods only update when manually deleted — the validator tests this automatically

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: log-agent
  labels:
    app: log-agent
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: log-agent
  updateStrategy:
    type: # TODO: Which strategy requires manual pod deletion to trigger updates?
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: log-agent
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: agent
        image: # TODO: Start with busybox:1.35 (will be updated to 1.36)
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Log agent v1 running"; sleep 30; done']
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: log-agent
  labels:
    app: log-agent
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: log-agent
  updateStrategy:
    type: OnDelete
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: log-agent
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: agent
        image: busybox:1.35
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Log agent v1 running"; sleep 30; done']
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for DaemonSet pods
echo "Waiting for log-agent pods..."
timeout=90
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  desired=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  ready=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [ "$desired" -gt 0 ] && [ "$ready" = "$desired" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

# Verify updateStrategy is OnDelete
strategy=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.spec.updateStrategy.type}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$strategy" = "OnDelete" ]; then
  echo "✓ updateStrategy is OnDelete"
else
  echo "✗ updateStrategy is '$strategy', expected OnDelete"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify initial image
img=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if echo "$img" | grep -q "busybox:1.35"; then
  echo "✓ Initial image is $img"
else
  echo "✗ Initial image is '$img', expected busybox:1.35"
  exit 1
fi

# Update image
kubectl set image daemonset/log-agent agent=busybox:1.36
echo "✓ Image updated to busybox:1.36 in spec"

# Verify pods did NOT auto-update (OnDelete behavior)
sleep 5
old_pods=$(kubectl get pods -l app=log-agent -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
all_old=true
while IFS= read -r podimg; do
  if echo "$podimg" | grep -q "1.36"; then
    all_old=false
    break
  fi
done <<< "$old_pods"

if $all_old; then
  echo "✓ No pods auto-updated — OnDelete strategy confirmed"
else
  echo "✗ A pod updated automatically — updateStrategy may not be OnDelete"
  exit 1
fi

# Manually delete one pod and verify it gets new image
first_pod=$(kubectl get pods -l app=log-agent --no-headers 2>/dev/null | head -1 | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -z "$first_pod" ]; then
  echo "✗ No pods found to delete"
  exit 1
fi

kubectl delete pod "$first_pod"
echo "Deleted pod $first_pod, waiting for replacement..."
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  new_ready=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  desired=$(kubectl get ds log-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}' 2>/dev/null || echo "1")
  if [ "$new_ready" = "$desired" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

# Find the new pod (different name) and check its image
new_pods=$(kubectl get pods -l app=log-agent -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
found_new=false
while IFS= read -r podimg; do
  if echo "$podimg" | grep -q "1.36"; then
    found_new=true
    break
  fi
done <<< "$new_pods"

if $found_new; then
  echo "✓ Replacement pod is running busybox:1.36 after manual deletion"
else
  echo "✗ No pod with busybox:1.36 found after manual deletion"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
